LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC'S MOTION TO LIFT ARBITRATION STAY IS DENIED. HOWEVER, THE REASONING FOR THE DENIAL OF THE MOTION IS VACATED. AND IN PLACE IT IS DENIED BASED ON THIS AGREEMENT OF WEST BUILDERS THAT THE INSURANCE POLICY CLAIM CAN BE AND WILL BE ARBITRATED SEPARATE FROM THE SLAB CLAIM AND THAT NOTHING ABOUT THE SLAB CLAIM WILL IN ANY WAY DELAY OR IMPEDE THE EXPEDITIOUS RESOLUTION OF THE INSURANCE POLICY ARBITRATION. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2021 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Museum Plaza Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift The Arbitration Stay To Confirm Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC's Motion To Lift The Arbitration Stay To Confirm Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Museum Plaza, LLC's unopposed motion to lift the arbitration stay, to confirm arbitration award and for entry of judgment thereon is granted. The Court will enter an order confirming the Final Award in its entirety (including the attached Term Sheet), and similarly will enter judgment pursuant to all of the terms of the Final Award and attached Term Sheet. This resolution should address Defendant and Cross-Complainant West Builders, Inc.'s "limited opposition," which it filed "solely to ensure that any judgment is subject to the covenant not to execute against West, as required under the Award."

Order signed. Separate judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Fredrick Hagen of the law offices of Berding & Weil LLP (925.838.2090) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Nicholas A. Merrell of the law offices of Varela Lee Metz & Guarino LLP (415.623.7000) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court modifies the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC'S Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay is DENIED. However, the reasoning for the denial of the motion is vacated. And in place it is DENIED based on this agreement of West Builders that the insurance policy claim can be and will be arbitrated separate from the slab claim and that nothing about the slab claim will in any way delay or impede the expeditious resolution of the insurance policy arbitration. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-10-2015 Continued To Mar-30-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay Or, Alternatively, To Compel West Builders, Inc. To Participate In Arbitration Proceedings And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant West Builders, Inc. And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
Off Calendar Aug-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC''''S Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay Or, Alternatively, To Compel West Builders, Inc. To Participate In Arbitration Proceedings And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant West Builders, Inc. And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To Ccp 128.5 is DENIED. Once a matter is referred to arbitration, the Court retains only "vestigial" jurisdiction over the matter, limited to "making the determination, upon conclusion of the arbitration proceedings, of whether there was an award on the merits (in which case the action at law should be dismissed because of the res judicata effects of the arbitration award) or not (at which point the action at law may resume to determine the rights of the parties). (Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1796.) If Museum Plaza does not wish to advance West''''s fees in order to preserve the arbitration, its remedy is to wait for the arbitrator to dismiss the matter based on West''''s non-payment of fees, then move to have the matter returned to the trial calendar on the basis that such a dismissal is not an "award" under California Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4. (See Cinel v. Cristopher (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 759.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
